content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be repurposed to selectively store only failed self-supervised exploration trajectories, tagging them by the type of unmet exploration curiosity that triggered the failed attempt to create a "curiosity failure buffer" that prunes redundant exploration. This buffer acts as a built-in curiosity regularizer that stops agents from repeatedly revisiting uninformative failed attempts, boosting exploration efficiency.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
